_doc: Supplemental cell-line entries for workhorse mouse lines NOT covered by MCCA (the MCCA cohort is heavily KRAS-GEMM-biased and misses the carcinogen-induced / spontaneous tumour lines that dominate everyday mouse-tumour work). Each entry is hand-curated from primary literature, with the source paper PMID under `litCitation`. Same field shape as metadata.json; `dataSource: 'Literature'` flags provenance for the UI. Anyone re-processing MCCA shouldn't touch this file.

_schemaNotes.immuneProfile: Per-line immune characterisation. Sourced from Mosely 2017 (PMID 28768203) where covered; TISMO / individual papers otherwise. Fields: tmbCategory (high/medium/low), msiStatus (high/stable/unknown), immunePhenotype (hot / inflamed / variable / cold), icbResponse (responsive / partial / resistant / untested), notes (free-text), source.
_schemaNotes.ncitDisease: NCI Thesaurus disease label for the cell line, from Cellosaurus.
_schemaNotes.cautions: Important caveats from Cellosaurus comment-list (variant heterogeneity, mis-identification risk, etc.).

lines.name: MC38
lines.litCitation: Corbett 1975 (Cancer Res 35:2434, PMID 1212137); exome / immune profile in Mosely 2017 (Cell Reports, PMID 28768203); Cellosaurus CVCL_B288
lines.mouseModelDetailed: 1,2-Dimethylhydrazine (DMH) induced colon adenocarcinoma in a female C57BL/6 mouse (Corbett 1975).

immuneProfile.tmbDetail: Highest TMB among the carcinogen-induced syngeneic panel; ~800+ non-synonymous mutations (Mosely 2017).
immuneProfile.msiDetail: Microsatellite-unstable, defective MMR phenotype consistent with DMH-induced mucosal damage.
immuneProfile.immunePhenotypeDetail: T-cell inflamed; substantial CD8+ tumour infiltration relative to the cold lines (B16-F10, 4T1, LL/2).
immuneProfile.icbResponseDetail: Anti-PD-1 and anti-PD-L1 sensitive — the canonical positive-control syngeneic model for checkpoint-blockade efficacy studies.

lines.distributor: Available via collaborators / Kerafast / Memorial Sloan Kettering bank.
cautions: Kerafast (MC38-K) and Leiden University Medical Center (MC38-L) variants differ substantially in mutations and mRNA expression — note your source when comparing studies (PMID 36969213).
Historical confusion with the rat line 'MCA38' / 'isolate 406'; the mitochondrial sequence KM820832.1 deposited as MCA38 is actually rat (PMID 25434994).
